reference_review:
POINTS:
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/OracleServerUtils.java:39] This does not seem to be used.
- [modules/integration/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/integration/client/FluoAdminImplIT.java:240] It would be good to use the Fluo API to read and write data because if for some reason Fluo API calls fail after remove, we want to know.  Later in the test, I think it would still be good to use an Accumulo scanner to ensure the table is empty though.
- [modules/integration/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/integration/client/FluoAdminImplIT.java:287] This loop could be replaced with `count = Iterables.size(scan)`  using Guava.  Also, I don't think entry will or should be null, so do not need to check that.

In addition to this Accumulo scan, it would also be nice to scan using Fluo.

POINTS:
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/client/Snapshot.java:36] ```suggestion
  /**
   * Sets the authorizations to use when reading data. This overrides anything that may have been set in the configuration used to create a FluoClient.
   *
   * @since 1.2.0
   */
  default Snapshot useScanTimeAuthorizations(Collection<String> authz) {
    throw new UnsupportedOperationException();
```
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/FluoConfiguration.java:522] This could return a list.

```suggestion
  /**
    *  Returns the authorizations configured for reading data or an empty list if none are configured.
    *
    * @since 1.2.0
    */
  public List<String> getAccumuloAuthorizations() {
```
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/client/FluoClient.java:83] If authorizations can be set in the FluoConfiguration, then this method is probably not needed.  FluoClients are always created from configuration and if the configuration has auths then the client can get them that way.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/impl/TransactionImpl.java:1583] There is also a parallel snapshots scanner used by this class that is a wrapper around the batch scanner. I am not sure if its gettings the correct auths passed to it.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/impl/TransactionImpl.java:1582] Do we want to use env.getAuthorizations()? What about auths set on the snapshot?
- [modules/integration-tests/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/integration/client/FluoClientIT.java:121] In addition to setting auths on client, would be nice to also test setting them on snapshot.
- [modules/integration-tests/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/integration/client/FluoClientIT.java:134] snapshot has multiple get methods, some use scanner and some use batch scanner.  Would be nice to test them all.

POINTS:
- [modules/integration-tests/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/integration/client/FluoAdminImplIT.java:302] Nice test.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/client/FluoAdminImpl.java:515] Does it need to be public?

SUMMARY:
I tried running stresso locally using this patch and it worked great.   While I had it running I ran a lot of commands and they worked as expected.

POINTS:

- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/ConfigCommand.java:66] Could have `@VisibleForTesting` annotation.  Also could `public` be removed?
- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoConfig.java:25] This is neat.
- [modules/command/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/command/ConfigCommandTest.java:59] Could have another test that sets two props.
- [modules/command/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoProgramTest.java:68] May make sense to restore these in an after class method.

PR DESCRIPTION:
First commit: Fix #871 Use regular jar for ITs module

    Move tests in the integration test module from src/test/java into
    src/main/java in order to create a regular jar instead of a test-jar in
    Maven. This fixes many issues, such as not having javadoc and source
    jars for the integration test module, as well as to simplify any
    dependency resolution issues with other modules or projects depending on
    the integration test jar.

Second commit: Fix test bugs and style issues found by checkstyle

    Fixes issues identified by checkstyle after fixing #871

reference_review:
POINTS:
- [modules/integration-tests/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/integration/impl/StochasticBankIT.java:63] The following file has constants for Accumulo props that Fluo uses.

./modules/accumulo/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/accumulo/util/AccumuloProps.java
- [modules/integration-tests/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/integration/impl/StochasticBankIT.java:197] Could be done as follow on work.   Would be nice to use org.apache.commons.math3.stat.descriptive.SummaryStatistics instead

POINTS:
- [modules/integration/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/integration/accumulo/TimeskippingIT.java:35] I would recommend replacing 60 with a constant or static method call.  The constant or static method could be placed in the ITBase class, which most ITs extend.    I think a static method would be nice because it could easily be modified in the future to get the timeout from a java system property.  

So could do something like the following where `getTestTimeout()` is declared in `ITBase`

```java
  @Rule
  public Timeout globalTimeout = Timeout.seconds(getTestTimeout());
```
- [modules/integration/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/integration/impl/WorkerIT.java:48] If using the static method, this could be modified to the following

```java
  @Rule
  public Timeout globalTimeout = Timeout.seconds( 2 * getTestTimeout());
```
- [modules/integration/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/integration/impl/GarbageCollectionIteratorIT.java:57] Nice
- [modules/integration/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/integration/impl/StochasticBankIT.java:57] Was this test causing problems with a timeout?

SUMMARY:
These changes look good.  I successfully ran `mvn verify` on the branch.  Also I ran the  `GarbageCollectionIteratorTest` in Eclipse with code coverage.  Running this test covered all code in ColumnBuffer (except the illegal arg case), so in my opinion a unit test is not needed.  But it certainly would not hurt to add one.

POINTS:
- [modules/accumulo/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/accumulo/iterators/ColumnBuffer.java:64] Accumulo Keys are mutable, so the safest thing would be to call the Key copy constructor like `key = new Key(k)`
- [modules/accumulo/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/accumulo/iterators/ColumnBuffer.java:62] Accumulo Values are mutable, would be safer to call the copy constructor here like `v = new Value(v)`
- [modules/accumulo/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/accumulo/iterators/GarbageCollectionIterator.java:229] The call to `new Value()` can be removed here since the ColumnBuffer add method will do that.
- [modules/accumulo/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/accumulo/iterators/GarbageCollectionIteratorTest.java:42] Why add these empty lines?
- [modules/accumulo/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/accumulo/iterators/ColumnBuffer.java:35] Changing this to an `ArrayList<byte[]>` would make this class more memory efficient.  This could be done as a follow on issue or in this PR.

POINTS:
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/client/scanner/ScannerBuilder.java:36] When javadoc is rendered, I think it usually puts the since info at the end.  As a result, its my personal pref is put the since tag at the end of the javadoc source.  Don't need to change if you don't agree.
- [modules/integration/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/integration/impl/FluoIT.java:467] Could remove `Bytes.of` and just have `over("d00001")`.  The calls to `Bytes.of` are from a time when support for Strings didn't exist everywhere in the API.   This was a pre-existing problem, does not have to be fixed in this PR.
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/client/scanner/ScannerBuilder.java:109] Need to change `rowPrefix` to `row`.
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/client/scanner/ScannerBuilder.java:105] This method scans a column prefix within an exact row.  So, need to change `rowPrefix` to `row`.   Also the javadoc should say something like `restrict the scanner to data in specified row`.
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/client/scanner/ScannerBuilder.java:106] Could say `restrict scanner to data that begins with specified column prefix`
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/client/scanner/ScannerBuilder.java:121] need to make same changes for rowPrefix to row

SUMMARY:
@jkosh44 thanks for taking time to work on this.  I made a few comments if you have time to make some more improvements.  I could only take a quick a look at the moment, I will give it another look later.

POINTS:
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/data/Bytes.java:227] This javadoc needs a `@since 1.2.0` tag
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/data/Bytes.java:195] This javadoc needs a `@since 1.2.0` tag
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/data/Bytes.java:274] This javadoc needs a `@since 1.2.0` tag
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/data/Bytes.java:286] This javadoc needs a `@since 1.2.0` tag
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/data/Bytes.java:229] Can you add a sanity check that `offset >= 0 && len >=0 && offset+len <= bytes.length`.  Could use `Preconditions.checkArgument` from guava.
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/data/Bytes.java:278] Should probably not return false when len or offset is negative.
- [modules/api/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/api/data/BytesTest.java:141] Would you be able to add test that check for the following error cases?
 
 * negative offset
 * negative length
 * offset + length > bytes.length

Could use Junit [expected attribute][1] for this.

[1]: http://www.mkyong.com/unittest/junit-4-tutorial-2-expected-exception-test/
- [modules/api/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/api/data/BytesTest.java:114] Would be nice to check different lengths and empty.

POINTS:
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/data/Bytes.java:195] This method does not need the since tag.
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/data/Bytes.java:234] Could create a private internal method `compareToUnchecked(byte[] bytes, int offset, int len)` that does not do these sanity checks.   Then methods that do not need the checking could call `compareToUnchecked()`.  For example `compareTo(Bytes other)`, `compareTo(byte[] bytes)`, and `contentEquals(byte[] bytes, int offset, int len)` do not need the checks.  

These changes could be made in this PR or as a follow on issue.
- [modules/api/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/api/data/BytesTest.java:137] A test for non-zero offsets would be nice.

```
  Assert.assertEquals(expected, people);
```

- [modules/api/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/api/data/BytesTest.java:282] I like this check to make sure no partial changes were made.
- [modules/api/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/api/data/BytesTest.java:285] some copyTo tests where end <= start would be nice.
